page_title: Visitor record: If you can’t apply online - Canada.ca

h2: Apply on paper to extend your stay (because of a disability)
Apply on paper to extend your stay (problem with the online application)

sections.heading: Apply on paper to extend your stay (because of a disability)
items: Follow the steps below to apply on paper to extend your stay as a visitor (get a visitor record).

sections.heading: Step 1: Get the application package
items: This package includes the application guide and all the forms you need to fill out.
Read the guide before you fill out the application form:
bullets: Instruction Guide [IMM 5551]

sections.heading: Documents
items: If you have questions about the forms, find answers to your questions in the Help Centre .
bullets: Document Checklist [IMM 5558] (opens in a new tab)
Application to Change Conditions, Extend my Stay or Remain in Canada as a Visitor or Temporary Resident Permit Holder [IMM 5708] (opens in a new tab)
Statutory Declaration of Common-Law Union [IMM 5409] (opens in a new tab)
Use of a Representative [IMM 5476] (opens in a new tab)

sections.heading: Step 2: Prepare your application package
items: Make sure you submit all the pages of the form.
Find out more about the barcode form .
bullets: Click the “Validate” button at the top or bottom of the form. This makes sure that you answered all the questions in the form and will help you avoid processing delays.
This makes sure that you answered all the questions in the form and will help you avoid processing delays.
A new page with barcodes and a signature line will automatically be added at the end of your form.
Print, sign and date the form. Print on white, bond-quality, non-glossy paper. Use a laser printer.
Print on white, bond-quality, non-glossy paper.
Use a laser printer.
Place the barcode page at the top of your package. If you have more than one package, place a separate barcode page on each one.

sections.heading: Step 3: Pay your application fees
items: In most cases, your fees will include processing fees for you and anyone you include on your application.
Our instruction guide can help you understand which fees apply to you and show you how to pay them.

sections.heading: Step 4: Submit your application
items: Submit your completed application to the address given in the application package. Make sure you include the fee receipt and all the necessary documents.

sections.heading: Alternate formats
items: If you can’t apply online or use the forms above because of a disability, you can ask for other formats including large print and braille. Contact the Client Support Centre (in Canada only) or submit a Web form for other formats.
